## Release — Blackhole v0.13.1

### ✨ What's new in v0.13.1

Patch release: two toolchain bug fixes plus a coverage-and-refactor batch,
all delivered by an autonomous backlog campaign (kaizen wave — issues #274–#282).
No config, API, or behavior changes for campaign users.

#### Fixes

- **`walkFilesAbs` no longer crashes on a dangling symlink** (#284) — the shared tree-walker used by `bun run build` and `bun run verify` called `fs.statSync` on every symlink, which throws `ENOENT` for a broken link. A single dangling symlink under `src/` (interrupted checkout, bad merge, local tooling) would abort the whole build/verify pipeline with a raw Node stack trace. Unresolvable symlinks are now skipped with a clear, attributed message.
- **`mergeDependsIntoBody` no longer deletes prose under `## Dependencies`** (#285) — when the campaign rewrote an issue's dependency set, any human- or tool-authored explanatory text under the `## Dependencies` heading was silently dropped. Non-dependency lines are now preserved; only the dependency lines themselves are rewritten.

#### Quality & refactor (internal)

- **`build.ts main()` split into per-target functions** (#287) — the 253-line, ~35-decision-point build orchestrator is now a thin sequencer over named per-target compile functions; build output is byte-identical.
- **`formatDashboard` split into per-section render helpers** (#290) — the campaign-status dashboard renderer drops under the complexity ceiling; output unchanged.
- **Shared `readJsonFile(path, label)` helper** (#292) — one labeled JSON read/parse in `scripts/lib/fs.ts` replaces a duplicate local helper and 7 bare `JSON.parse(readFileSync(...))` sites, so malformed campaign state now surfaces with the file name instead of an unlabeled `SyntaxError`.
- **Dead build step removed** (#288) — dropped a redundant deprecated `bun run build --gemini` pass from the install hook.

#### Test coverage

- **`normalizeTag` release-gate validator** (#283) — the sole guard before an irreversible `git tag`/`git push` now has direct accept/reject tests.
- **`checkFixtures` (V-SCHEMA-01)** (#286) — the fixture-schema drift guard's per-fixture validators are extracted and unit-tested.
- **`validate-worker-json` hook-input fallback** (#289) — the Stop-hook transcript-tail fallback path (byte-offset read + two-path parse) is now covered.

### Verification

`bun test`: 429/429 pass (+30 vs v0.13.0). `bun run verify`: 27/27 checks pass.

---
